title: Deep Dive Q&A
description: Build layered questions and answer scaffolds for review, explanation, or teaching.

placeholders.topic: For example: React Server Components, vector databases, payment risk control

hintDescription: If you are explaining {{topic}} to {{audience}}, start with why it matters before how it works.
noticeDescription: A {{depth}} question ladder has been generated around {{topic}}.

quick.0: What core problem does {{topic}} actually solve?
quick.1: If we skipped it, where would the team likely pay the price first?
quick.2: What is the main cost of adopting it?
quick.3: What should the first pilot look like in a real project?

deep.0: What assumptions sit behind {{topic}}, and why do they hold?
deep.1: How does the core mechanism work, and how does data or state move through it?
deep.2: Compared with common alternatives, where is the real tradeoff?
deep.3: What boundaries and risks should be clarified before adopting it here?

expert.0: Which assumptions around {{topic}} break first when scale or constraints change?
expert.1: If the critical path runs into performance or consistency trouble, which signals would you inspect first?
expert.2: What migration strategy is most realistic in a legacy system?
expert.3: If this became platform capability, how would you define boundaries and ownership?

team.0: Start with the problem context, then show which repeated work or uncertainty it removes.
team.1: Explain the core mechanism through one concrete data flow instead of jumping straight to abstraction.
team.2: State the gains and the costs together so the team can judge whether it is worth adopting.
team.3: Close with pilot scope, success metrics, and rollback conditions.

stakeholder.0: Frame it first in terms of efficiency, stability, or cost before technical detail.
stakeholder.1: Use one or two concrete business cases to show why the investment matters.
stakeholder.2: Translate risk into time, quality, or resource cost so decisions are easier.
stakeholder.3: Offer a staged path to show this is not a rewrite bet.

learner.0: Start with a plain-language definition, then add an example they can picture.
learner.1: Break the mechanism into steps so too many new concepts do not arrive at once.
learner.2: Compare it to something familiar to lower the learning barrier.
learner.3: End with a small exercise they can validate on their own.
